items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment documentation is available
Previous inspection reports are checked
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Operator is certified(for the inspected crane type/model) and licensed
Load rating chart is applicable to the configured boom and is legible
An operating manual is available (English)
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Crane is painted safety yellow
Crane is painted safety yellow-and-black stripes on offshore facilities
Rated capacity of crane is marked(Arabic & English)
A fire extinguisher with minimum rating of 10 BC is installed in the cab or at the machinery housing
Electrical apparatus is working correctly without excessive dirt, deterioration or moisture accumulation
Control levers are all operable from the operator station
Control pedals are functioning correctly
All controls are labeled as to their functions and within reach of operator
Guards are fitted and secured to cover exposed moving parts
Lubrication chart is available on the side boom
All moving parts that require lubrication are lubricated and accessible
Tires and wheels are in condition and as recommended inflation pressure
Boom angle indicator is functioning correctly
Boom hoist shut off is functioning correctly
Load indicator is functioning correctly
Capacity indicator is functioning correctly
Gasoline, diesel, electric, or other power plants to be checked for improper performance or noncompliance with other applicable standards
Emergency shut down is operating satisfactorily
Engine throttle is functioning satisfactorily
Battery is secure and ventilated
Engine and Fuel level gauges are functioning correctly
Exhaust system is not corroded, and is guarded or insulated where necessary to prevent personal contact with hot surfaces
Engine oil level is adequate.
Hydraulic/Pneumatic systems are not leaking
Oil and fuel tanks leaks are checked
Hydraulic/Pneumatic systems are not leaking (pumps, valves, lines, etc.).
Hook assembly labeling and manufacturer data is clearly marked
Hook's weight is clearly marked/printed on the hook
Safe working load of hook is clearly marked on the item
Hook does not show defects such as nicks, cracks and gouges
Hook is not bent or twisted
Hook is not distorted in the throat opening

• Max. allowable throat opening is 5% not to exceed 1/4 in. (6 mm) , or as per manufacturer recommendations Maximum wear in the hook bowl is not exceeding 10%(compared to new hook) or as per manufacturer recommendations
Hook is not cracked, gouged or shows nicks
Hook can lock(if it is a self-locking hook)
Hook latch is operative
All critical parts are not cracked, broken, bent, corroded welds or excessively worn.
worn, cracked, or distorted parts such as pins, bearings, shafts, gears, rollers, and locking and clamping devices are checked
Boom bushings and pins are not worn and are secure
Machinery shall be stationary while lubricants are being applied and protection provided

When using boom hoist reeving or hydraulic cylinder system with maximum load capacity suspended, the boom hoist mechanism shall be capable of raising or lowering the boom, stopping boom motion, and holding
Sheave grooves are free from surface defects and lubricated
Rope Carrying sheaves have close-fittings guards or other suitable devices
Lower block sheaves have close-fittings guards
All moving parts of the side boom tractor for which lubrication is specified, including rope and chain, should be regularly lubricated
Bearings and bushings are checked and lubricated
Boom stops are fitted and undamaged
Bearings and bushings are undamaged, secure and lubricated

DRUM 1 LOAD HOIST MECHANISM 6.1.1 Load hoist brake function is satisfactory 6.1.2 Load lifting and lowering operations are satisfactory Two full wraps, at least, of rope remain on the drum when the hook is in 6.1.3 its extreme low working position Rope ends are anchored to the drum by clamps or as per manufacturer 6.1.4 recommendations Drums are provided with a guidance or other means to prevent rope 6.1.5 from jumping off the drum Power controlled lowering is operational and meets the speed and load 6.1.6 criteria as per manufacturers specifications Positive braking means is available, controllable by the operator, to 6.1.7 prevent drum rotation in the lowering direction

DRUM 2 BOOM HOIST MECHANISM 6.2.1 Boom hoist mechanism (raising and lowering) is properly operating 6.2.2 Boom hoist brake and clutch are correctly operating 6.2.3 Boom hoist brake and clutch have adjustments to compensate for wear Locking pawl and ratchet is in good working condition to prevent 6.2.4 inadvertent lowering of the boom Two full wraps, at least, of rope remain on the drum when the hook is in 6.2.5 its extreme low working position Rope ends are anchored to the drum by clamps or as per manufacturer 6.2.6 recommendations Drums are provided with a guidance or other means to prevent rope 6.2.7 from jumping off the drum Broken wires— in running ropes, six randomly distributed broken wires 6.2.8 in six rope diameters or three broken wires in one strand in six rope diameters Broken wires—(guys), including end connections, six randomly 6.2.9 distributed broken wires in six rope diameters or three broken wires in one strand in six rope diameters Removal criteria for rope replacement, Reduction from nominal diameter 6.2.10 of more than 5% The rope does not have kinking, crushing, birdcaging, evidence of heat 6.2.11 damage, ubstranding, core corrosion, main strand displacement or any other damages Boom hoist cylinder and associated hydraulic system is operating 6.2.12 correctly 6.2.13 Boom hoist cylinder seals are not leaking

HAND TRACK There is no worn, cracked, or distorted parts such as pins, bearings, 7.1.1 shafts, gears, rollers, and locking and clamping devices 7.1.2 Track rollers and followers are turning and not loose, worn or seized
items.referenceStandard: ASME B30.14 Sec. 2.1.3 (d) ASME B30.14 Sec. 2.1.3 (d)

items.acceptanceCriteria: FUNCTIONS 7.2.1 Propelling forward is accepted 7.2.2 Propelling backward is accepted 7.2.3 Transmission gearbox functioning well and gears are operational 7.2.4 Steering to the right is operational 7.2.5 Steering to the left is operational 7.2.6 Travel locks are operational and remain in engagement 7.2.7 All safety limit devices are operational

DRIVERS CAB 8.1.1 Cab glass is made of safety glazing material Visibility on the boom side of the cab such that the uppermost point of 8.1.2 the boom is visible at all times. 8.1.3 Road mirrors are fitted and undamaged 8.1.4 Fire extinguisher is available in the cab (minimum rating is 10BC)

ENGINE/DRIVE TRAIN 8.2.1 Ignition system is operating correctly 8.2.2 Throttle is operating correctly 8.2.3 Battery is secure and ventilated 8.2.4 Gauges are in good working condition Engine exhaust system is guarded or insulated where necessary to 8.2.5 prevent personal contact with hot surfaces Engine exhaust gases are piped to the outside of the cab and 8.2.6 discharged in a direction away from the operator

FRONT 9.1.1 Tires and rims are undamaged and serviceable Means to hold all stabilizers in the retracted position while traveling and 9.1.2 in the extended position is provided. 9.1.3 Outrigger pads are securely fitted and undamaged 9.1.4 Pad pins are locked and undamaged 9.1.5 Extension cylinders are working correctly and undamaged 9.1.6 Vertical cylinders are working correctly and undamaged

REAR 9.2.1 Tires and rims are undamaged and serviceable Means to hold all stabilizers in the retracted position while traveling and 9.2.2 in the extended position is provided. 9.2.3 Outrigger pads are securely fitted and undamaged 9.2.4 Pad pins are locked and undamaged 9.2.5 Extension cylinders are working correctly and undamaged 9.2.6 Vertical cylinders are working correctly and undamaged

TIP SECTION 10.1.1 Boom tip section is undamaged, has no cracks or distortion 10.1.2 boom extension cylinder is properly working with no signs of leaks 10.1.3 Guide sheaves are not worn and are well lubricated 10.1.4 Sheave grooves are free from surface defects 10.1.5 Bushings/bearings are not worn and are well lubricated 10.1.6 Guards are fitted and secured to cover exposed moving parts Rope end is anchored and rope clamp is tightened in accordance with 10.1.7 the manufacturers recommendations

COUNTER WEIGHT Counterweights is used in accordance with the manufacturer’s 10.2.1 specifications 10.2.2 Hydraulic hoses have no leaks Complete counterweight and arm assembly shows no cracks or 10.2.3 deformation
